subject: docs(claude): promote Communication to top-level section
body:
Promote the five-bullet "Communication Style" block (previously sitting
between Suggested Implementations and Testing) to a proper top-level
"## Communication" section placed right after "## Principles" so the
rules sit near the other prescriptive sections at the top of the file
rather than buried in the middle. Expand the content to match the
glow-props source of truth: peer-to-peer framing, explicit anti-
sycophancy / anti-hedging rules, proper-solutions-only mandate, and
the "always ask at least one question" minimum bar.

Drop the two now-duplicate bullets from AI Notes:
  - "ASK before assuming on bug reports" (covered by "Ask before
    assuming")
  - "Communication style: Direct, concise..." (superseded by the
    full section)

Part 2 of the original task referenced a `# READ AND FOLLOW THE
PROCESS...` top-of-file header line that doesn't exist in repo-tor's
CLAUDE.md — this repo uses per-section `### REMINDER:` lines as the
reinforcement pattern, so I added a matching one
(`### REMINDER: READ AND FOLLOW THE COMMUNICATION RULES EVERY TIME`)
instead of fabricating a header line the rest of the doc wouldn't
reference.

Parts 4 (ICON_CACHE_BUST tripwire test) and 5 (install-modal "icon
looks outdated?" collapsible) from the task description were already
landed on main by commit 36656b1 on a prior branch — no work needed
here; the scripts/__tests__/icon-cache-bust.test.mjs source-level
assertions pass in the current tree.

Tags: documentation, claude-md, communication
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: communication-rules-alignment
Semver: patch
